reply: Useful because it shows Claude Code as an operating routine, not a prompt trick. The next frontier is review and recovery, not just longer commands.
The interesting part is the single operator serving many stores. For Shopify, agents matter when they compress repeated ops, not when they demo well.
That 3-5 supervision ceiling feels real. The bottleneck is not launching more coding agents, it is knowing which ones deserve attention.
The org-structure framing is underrated. Once agents share goals and review paths, the company starts looking more like a queueing system.
Repos change the whole Claude Code experience. Skills and memory make it feel less like autocomplete and more like a repeatable workspace.
The crazy part is how fast Codex shifted from editor helper to full build surface. Browser use and review loops make that jump feel real.
本地跑这套最吸引我的不是省钱，而是权限和材料都留在自己机器上。企业内部会很在意这一点。
Control is the right emphasis here. Creators will accept AI faster when it stays inside their existing Unity loop instead of replacing the craft.
Auto-review is the feature I would watch most. Full browser use is powerful, but the review layer decides whether people trust longer runs.
Paid ads are a great test for agents because the loop has clear inputs, outputs, and measurable feedback. That makes automation less hand-wavy.
Seeing MCP, Skills, Desktop, and Claude Code come from one Labs culture explains a lot. They were building the workflow surface piece by piece.
我也更信 goal-driven。没有目标和验收标准，Codex 跑得越久，偏航成本反而越高。
Hermes sounds compelling because it learns from past conversations, not just the current tab. Cross-session memory is where agents start to compound.
永久记忆最有用的地方是少解释背景。很多任务不是缺模型能力，是每次都从零开始太浪费。
The pitfall section is probably the most valuable part. /goal is powerful only when the objective and stopping criteria are tight.
The /goal shift matters because it makes Codex accountable to an outcome. I am more interested in failure recovery than the happy path.
The execute-plans part is the real shift. Once tools and permissions are attached, agents become process workers, not just answer engines.
Weekly builds are a good filter. GPT-5.5 plus image generation will surface who can turn model capability into a real product loop.
实时插入消息对长任务很实用。跑到一半能补上下文，比事前把 prompt 写到完美更接近真实开发。
五阶段拆法适合新手，因为它把“想法”先变成可检查的材料。直接开写代码，agent 反而更容易跑偏。
Voice reaching the full agent is a bigger deal than it sounds. Dictation only helps when the spoken instruction can travel through the whole run.
卖 Agent 运行时这条线会越来越清楚。沙箱、状态、权限、调度，都是企业不想自己重复造的部分。
半天做出完整玩法很惊人。我最想看的是素材、地图、战斗、图鉴能不能被同一套流程持续迭代。
Kanban is a natural UI for multi-agent work. Dependencies and handoffs are where most orchestration demos usually fall apart.
Most teams rush from prompting to agents and skip the workflow layer. That middle step is where tasks become measurable and repeatable.
代码 Wiki 如果能随仓库变化自动更新，价值很大。最怕的是文档漂亮，但落后代码两周。
Cheap models are useful as long as the routing is honest. I would still reserve the hardest coding and review paths for the strongest model.
Agree with this. Design systems fail when they become static docs; AI tools need to respect tokens, variants, and messy handoff paths.
这套组合很像个人工作台：本地模型做轻任务，Codex 处理长任务，再用设计工具补视觉。分工比全塞一个工具稳。
开启方式写得很实用。/goal 最大的价值是让 Codex 记住终点，但验收条件还是得人先想清楚。
PPT 这类任务很适合新版 Codex：能预览、能改局部、还能补图。排版反馈链路短了很多。
A personal AI device only makes sense if it owns context and permissions. Otherwise it is just another screen with a better assistant.
Render stall 这个点很细但很重要。工具再强，只要输出过程卡顿，人就很难把它当成可持续工作台。
TypeScript parity matters for adoption. A lot of agent products live in web stacks, so SDK friction directly changes what teams build.
模型接入面越宽，Hermes 越像统一控制台。后面难点是不同模型能力和成本的路由策略。
Repeatability is the important word. Coding agents become useful when the workflow can be rerun, inspected, and improved after each task.
Higher limits change behavior more than people admit. Once the ceiling moves, developers start giving Claude Code bigger chunks of work.
把十年设计经验压进 Skill，这个方向很对。对普通人来说，最缺的不是会生成，而是有一套审美约束。
步骤写清楚了，很适合转给刚升级的人。/goal 不难开，难的是给它一个够明确的结束条件。
Dream 这个名字很妙。它把 memory 从被动记录推进到主动整理，后面会影响 agent 怎么学会长期协作。
gstack 的定位挺清楚：先判断想法值不值，再把执行团队拉起来。创业场景里，这两个阶段不能混在一起。
围绕架构知识展开工作是对的。以后不是把文档写完给 AI，而是让它在执行中持续更新上下文。
Exposing the agent as an MCP tool is a smart boundary. It keeps orchestration flexible without exploding the number of tiny tool endpoints.
The diary analogy lands because memory failures feel mundane, not dramatic. The agent forgets small context, then the whole run drifts.
Hermes 这轮有意思的是不只加工具，而是在补记忆、协作和技能生态。它开始像一个开发者工作台。
Autoreason is a useful framing for strategy work. When there is no test to pass, the review loop has to make uncertainty visible.
Trading workflows are a hard test for Claude because live data, rules, and execution risk all collide. The audit trail matters as much as speed.
把网站链接丢进去直接生成宣传片，这条链路很适合产品冷启动。半小时能出可看的版本，测试成本会降很多。
Memory methods are only useful if they survive the messy middle of a run. The 4,096-token example is a good reminder of where things break.
垂直行业会很快出现这种 agent matrix。售前、设计、施工、售后放在一起，价值比单点工具大很多。
